search_queries_used: site:ese-hormones.org adrenal incidentaloma 2023 guideline imaging hormone assessment follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic benign, malignant or hormone-producing classification
automatic hormonal testing, imaging interval, surgery or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Imaging and hormonal interpretation require exact lesion, assay and clinical context.
All tests, procedures and referrals remain clinician decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with an incidentally identified adrenal mass; pregnancy, youth, frailty, known malignancy and hereditary syndromes require additional review.
setting_applicability: Endocrine and multidisciplinary incidentaloma result review.
UAE_applicability: International ESE guidance transfers partially; UAE radiology, endocrine laboratory, oncology, surgery and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: The 2023 ESE guideline exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2023 revised ESE guideline remains current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ESE hormone-assessment section supports associated symptom, relevant-negative, patient-concern, measured vital-sign, focused endocrine assessment and clinician-reviewed endocrine laboratory documentation for an incidental adrenal lesion without inferring hormone excess.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinical signs of hormone excess, blood pressure and hypertension, diabetes context, recommendation-qualified hormonal workup, and electrolytes or hypokalaemia context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact imaging section supports clinician review of previous endocrine correspondence and lesion-imaging context, while the generic field remains conditional and does not classify malignancy risk.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports imaging-report review, CT or MRI context, lesion characteristics, qualified follow-up imaging, and multidisciplinary discussion.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact follow-up section supports a clinician-entered plan, completed specialist discussion and arranged follow-up only when documented; it does not recommend surgery, testing or an interval.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports qualified clinician-arranged multidisciplinary review, referral, hormonal reassessment, and imaging follow-up without prescribing timing universally.

unresolved_source_gaps: Lesion size, laterality, imaging phenotype, interval change, specific hormones, comorbidities, interpretation, diagnosis, surgery, treatment and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
